transcript: GM GM everyone, I am Charlie from Nansen. Today I will be introducing a new way to trade. Nansen's mission has always been to surface the signal, and to create winners. What this means is that we help investors in crypto to grow their portfolios. We've been doing this for the last six years, and we've enabled more than 1 million users to win onchain. We do this by labeling wallets. We have more than 450 million wallets labeled, including funds, entities, KOLs, angel investors, and more. We have lightning fast analytics. We provide data in real time across every chain that matters. Of course, including Solana. But the space is changing. Investing is changing. The way that you surface the signal is changing, and the way that you will trade is also changing. Now this is a big question: What will trading actually look like in 2030? I am confident that dashboards are dead in 2030. Now this is a big statement. Nansen is known for its dashboards. We provide dashboards to investors to help them win. But, I am confident that instead, you will chat with data instead of having to navigate confusing dashboards, tables, and widgets to make actionable investment decisions. I believe this is what the future of trading will look like. On the go, 24/7, being able to interface with an agent that can execute for you. Meet Nansen AI. Nansen AI is an AI-powered mobile app that is available now on the iOS and Android stores, and it gives you hedge fund research team level insights on the go. You can interface with Nansen's data directly with natural language, rather than needing to surf through all these different dashboards yourself. You can ask questions like 'Why is Solana up today?', 'What are smart money investors buying and selling in real time?' And you can get access to these 450 million addresses directly from your pocket. Here's an example of what it looks like: 'Find top 5 tokens on Solana in the last 24 hours based on smart money activity.' And these insights are delivered directly to you. You have alpha on a silver platter that you can take action on immediately. But, there is one more thing. That helps you to surface the signal. But what about executing on that signal as well? Today, I'm excited to introduce a new way to trade. Nansen has now introduced embedded wallet functionality into our mobile app and also our web app, allowing you to be able to trade directly within Nansen. You can now surface the signal and execute on it all in the same place. It gives you the opportunity to discover opportunities in an agentic way, and perform due diligence as well. We have the built-in wallet functionality and also execution all in the same wheelhouse, and it's entirely onchain and non-custodial. What Cursor did for coding, Nansen will do for trading. This concept is called Vibe Trading. And Nansen is the pioneer. We plug in to various DEX aggregators like Jupiter, OKX DEX, Li.Fi, and our embedded wallet system is powered by Privy. This enables us to give the best price execution. It's on the go, 24/7, and again, entirely onchain. Today you can download Nansen AI entirely for free on the iOS store and the Android store as well. The only thing between you and 1 million dollars is a great prompt. Thank you.

sources.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.2 is here: what enterprises need to know
sources.snippet:
OpenAI's GPT-5.2 is here: what enterprises need to know | VentureBeat
[](https://venturebeat.com/)
[Newsletters](https://venturebeat.com/newsletters)
[All Posts](https://venturebeat.com/)
# OpenAI's GPT-5.2 is here: what enterprises need to know
[Carl Franzen](https://venturebeat.com/author/carlfranzen)December 11, 2025
![OpenAI researchers running around control room with 5.2 on screen](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F3V50gYllm3mWHfplVg6RxG%2Fa06f74823800f27a945af5a27c04bb5c%2FaXhBsF1CKZZnrxoHyH4hN.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=85)
Credit: VentureBeat made with Reve via Fal.ai
[](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=venturebeat.com)
The rumors were true: OpenAI on Thursday announced the release of its new frontier large language model (LLM) family,[**GPT-5.2**](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-2/).
It comes at a pivotal moment for the AI pioneer, which has faced intensifying pressure since rival[Google’s Gemini 3 LLM seized the top spot](https://venturebeat.com/ai/google-unveils-gemini-3-claiming-the-lead-in-math-science-multimodal-and)on major third-party performance leaderboards and many key benchmarks last month, though OpenAI leaders stressed in a press briefing that the timing of this release had been discussed and worked on well in advance of the release of Gemini 3.
OpenAI describes GPT-5.2 as its "most capable model series yet for professional knowledge work," aiming to reclaim the performance crown with significant gains in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows.
"It’s our most advanced frontier model and the strongest yet in the market for professional use," Fidji Simo, OpenAI’s CEO of Applications, said during a press briefing today. "We designed 5.2 to unlock even more economic value for people. It's better at creating spreadsheets, building presentations, writing code, perceiving images, understanding long context, using tools, and handling complex, multi-step projects."
GPT-5.2 features a massive 400,000-token context window —allowing it to ingest hundreds of documents or large code repositories at once —and a 128,000 max output token limit, enabling it to generate extensive reports or full applications in a single go.
The model also features a knowledge cutoff of August 31, 2025, ensuring it is up-to-date with relatively recent world events and technical documentation. It explicitly includes "Reasoning token support," confirming the underlying architecture uses the chain-of-thought processing popularized by the "o1" series.
### **The 'Code Red' Reality Check**
The release arrives following**[*The Information*'s report](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/openai-ceo-declares-code-red-combat-threats-chatgpt-delays-ads-effort)of an emergency "Code Red" directive to OpenAI staff from CEO Sam Altman to improve ChaTGPT —a move reportedly designed to mobilize resources following the "quality gap" exposed by Gemini 3.[*The Verge*](https://www.theverge.com/report/838857/openai-gpt-5-2-release-date-code-red-google-response)similarly reported on the timing of GPT-5.2's release ahead of the official announcement.
During the briefing, OpenAI executives acknowledged the directive but pushed back on the narrative that the model was rushed solely to answer Google.
"It is important to note this has been in the works for many, many months," Simo told reporters. She clarified that while the "Code Red" helped focus the company, it wasn't the sole driver of the timeline.
"We announced this Code Red to really signal to the company that we want to marshal resources in one particular area... but that's not the reason it's coming out this week in particular."
Max Schwarzer, lead of OpenAI's post-training team, echoed this sentiment to dispel the idea of a panic launch. "We've been

# Meta shifts some metaverse investments to AI smart glasses
7 days ago
ShareSave
Osmond ChiaBusiness reporter
ShareSave
![](https://static.files.bbci.co.uk/bbcdotcom/web/20251203-121739-f954f14c69-web-2.35.1-2/grey-placeholder.png)![Getty Images Mark Zuckerberg, chief executive officer of Meta, wears a pair of Meta Ray-Ban Display AI glasses while speaking on stage ](https://ichef.bbci.co.uk/news/480/cpsprodpb/b950/live/f14ab5f0-d18b-11f0-abf3-97535f35adb4.jpg.webp)Getty Images
Meta boss Mark Zuckerberg showing off a pair of Meta's smart glasses
Meta is shifting some of its investments in the metaverse to AI glasses and wearables, hoping to capitalise on the "momentum" in that segment, a company spokesperson has said.
Over recent years, Meta has poured billions of dollars to build the metaverse, which lets people to interact in a virtual reality. However, the tech giant has struggled to convince investors of the viability of the nascent technology.
Bloomberg first reported on Thursday that Meta would cut its metaverse investment by as much as 30%. Its shares climbed more than 3.4% following the news.
The strategic shift to the Metaverse was the reason why Facebook changed its name to Meta in 2021.
"We aren't planning any broader changes than that," said Meta's spokesperson, without commenting on whether the shift in investments will result in layoffs.
Now, the company is seeking to build on an early advantage on AI-enabled glasses, after the positive reception to[the latest models launched in September](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y599yz0l3o).
The glasses feature a small display within the lens that can describe what it sees and even translate text. That feature is widely seen as a breakthrough that enhances the technology's usefulness while making the design more compact.
Many players in the industry, including firms in China, have also joined the race to build smart glasses and wearable technology.
Meta has struggled in recent years to convince investors about the metaverse. Many have been sceptical over its vision for an immersive digital space and the demand for virtual reality (VR) headsets, which are key to that technology.
The company has invested heavily in such headsets and its metaverse platform,[Horizon Worlds](https://www.bbc.com/news/technology-59609996), where users can interact as avatars.
Demand for other technologies, especially artificial intelligence (AI), have also surged ahead while Meta prioritised the metaverse.
The firm has recently shifted its focus to building large AI models, like the software integrated into WhatsApp, and developing smart gadgets like its new glasses.
[
## Facebook owner unveils new AI-powered smart glasses
](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y599yz0l3o)

sources.title: Adobe plugs Photoshop, Acrobat tools into ChatGPT
sources.snippet:
[Skip to main content](https://www.reuters.com/www.reuters.com#main-content)

[Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv](https://www.reuters.com/differentiator/)

Adobe logo is seen in this illustration created on February 16, 2025. REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration/File Photo [Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab](https://www.reutersconnect.com/item/illustration-shows-adobe-logo/dGFnOnJldXRlcnMuY29tLDIwMjU6bmV3c21sX1JDMklXQ0FVTEQ0Sg%3D%3D/?utm_medium=rcom-article-media&utm_campaign=rcom-rcp-lead)

Dec 10 (Reuters) - Adobe [(ADBE.O), opens new tab](https://www.reuters.com/markets/companies/ADBE.O) is integrating Photoshop, Adobe Express and Acrobat apps into ChatGPT, it said on Wednesday, allowing users to edit images, design graphics and manage PDFs within the OpenAI-owned chatbot.

The move reflects a broader push by software makers to tie everyday tools into conversational AI platforms and tap into more users while reducing the need to switch between different applications.

Sign up [here.](https://www.reuters.com/undefined?location=article-paragraph&redirectUrl=%2Ftechnology%2Fadobe-plugs-photoshop-acrobat-tools-into-chatgpt-2025-12-10%2F)

Adobe declined to comment on the financial terms with OpenAI and said the integration was aimed at showcasing its flagship to new users, who will need to register with Adobe to use them in ChatGPT.

The rollout will bring many of the popular features that are available across Adobe's applications to ChatGPT's more than 800 million weekly active users, expanding Adobe's reach as it ramps up efforts to adapt to AI-driven changes in professional design markets.

It is also a step up for Adobe at a time when demand rises for faster, chat-based interactions that appeal to both beginners and skilled creators.

Aimed at simplifying common creative and productivity tasks, Adobe said users can type a request in ChatGPT such as fine-tuning photos, creating a graphic, animating designs or summarizing a PDF, and trigger the corresponding Adobe tool without leaving the chat interface.

Photoshop, Acrobat and Adobe Express are free to use within ChatGPT starting Wednesday across ChatGPT desktop, web and iOS. Adobe Express for ChatGPT is already live on Android, with Photoshop and Acrobat support for Android expected to follow soon.

The move builds on Adobe's announcement from [late October](https://www.reuters.com/business/media-telecom/adobe-reworks-its-apps-ai-assistants-pursues-chatgpt-integration-2025-10-28/), when the company overhauled its video and image editing tools to allow users to execute tasks via conversational AI assistants.

Reporting by Arnav Mishra in Bengaluru and Stephen Nellis in San Francisco

A bioluminescent neural network growing from the cracks symbolizes the rise of this new, organic, and intelligent trading agent. I included the diegetic text '>EXECUTE' blinking on a screen fragment to highlight the platform's core shift from passive analysis to active trading.

From the deep cracks, a vibrant, electric-blue bioluminescent neural network pulsates with light, its tendrils wrapping around the dead hardware. This glowing AI is the only light source, casting dramatic, high-contrast shadows. On a large shard of glass near the center, a ghostly green command prompt from the old system blinks faintly: `>EXECUTE`.

From the deep, jagged cracks splitting the dead hardware, a vibrant, electric-blue bioluminescent neural network pulsates with an ethereal light, its intricate tendrils gracefully wrapping around the broken components. This glowing AI serves as the sole light source, creating an extreme chiaroscuro effect, casting dramatic, long, high-contrast shadows that stretch into the oppressive darkness, emphasizing the epic scale and depth of the scene. The volumetric lighting from the bioluminescence creates a mysterious, high-contrast cyber-noir atmosphere. On a large, prominent shard of glass positioned near the center of the frame, a ghostly green command prompt from the defunct old system faintly blinks, deeply etched and shimmering: `>EXECUTE`. The composition emphasizes verticality, with ample negative space above the towering, broken structure, highlighting its monumental presence. The shattered glass exhibits intricate fracture patterns and reflective surfaces. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, ultra-realistic lighting, deep shadows, cinematic composition.

editorial.headline: Nansen Launches AI Execution Terminal, Declares "Dashboards Are Dead"
editorial.standfirst: The analytics giant pivots from passive observation to active "Vibe Trading," integrating direct execution to close the gap between signal and trade.
key_takeaways: Nansen AI mobile app launches with natural language interface, replacing complex dashboards.
Native execution integrated via **Jupiter**, **OKX DEX**, and **Li.Fi**; embedded wallets powered by **Privy**.
New "Vibe Trading" thesis aims to replicate **Cursor**'s impact on coding for the crypto trading workflow.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Nansen has fundamentally altered its strategic direction at Breakpoint 2025, moving beyond its roots as a pure analytics provider to become an active trading terminal. By introducing embedded wallet functionality and direct execution, the firm is attempting to capture value further down the user funnel. The launch of the Nansen AI mobile app represents a bet that the future of institutional and retail trading lies not in staring at charts, but in conversational interfaces driven by agents.

The company’s explicitly stated thesis—"Dashboards are dead"—challenges the prevailing UX orthodoxy of the crypto industry. For years, complexity was a moat; Nansen is now wagering that accessibility is the new alpha. By allowing users to query 450 million labeled wallets using natural language (e.g., "What are smart money investors buying?"), Nansen is democratizing hedge-fund caliber data. This shift mirrors the broader tech trend toward agentic workflows, positioning the app as a "Cursor for trading" where the user provides intent and the AI handles the technical overhead.

Crucially, this is an infrastructure play as much as a UI update. The integration of **Privy** for non-custodial embedded wallets and aggregators like **Jupiter**, **OKX DEX**, and **Li.Fi** transforms Nansen from a map into a vehicle. Investors can now discover signal, perform due diligence, and execute trades without leaving the application. For VCs and developers, this consolidation of the "discovery-to-execution" loop suggests a future where liquidity fragmentation is solved not just by aggregators, but by the research tools that sit upstream of the trade.
